headline: GOLD NEAR $4,750 ALL-TIME HIGH — JPMorgan Raises Year-End Target to $6,300; Central Banks Still Buying
snippet: Gold is trading near $4,748/oz as of April 14, 2026, up 46% year-over-year. JPMorgan revised its year-end target to $6,300/oz, with central banks expected to purchase ~755 tonnes in 2026, still elevated vs. pre-2022 averages.

headline: $2.2 BILLION DEAL — G Mining Ventures Acquires G2 Goldfields at 72% Premium, Targets 500 koz/yr Guyana Gold Hub
snippet: G Mining Ventures (GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal worth ~$2.2B at a 72% premium. The deal consolidates two adjacent Oko district projects in Guyana and unlocks over C$1B in synergies, targeting 500+ koz/yr LOM average production.

headline: SILVER HEADING FOR SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT — Silver Institute Projects 67 Moz Shortfall; Physical Investment Up 20%
snippet: The Silver Institute forecasts a 67 Moz supply deficit in 2026, the sixth consecutive annual shortfall. Physical silver investment is projected to jump 20% to 227 Moz — a three-year high — as elevated prices and macro uncertainty reignite retail demand.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m @ 0.83 g/t Au at Diablillos Phase VI — Argentina DFS On Track for Q2 2026
snippet: AbraSilver's Phase VI drill program at Diablillos, Argentina returned a highlight intercept of 171m @ 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag from 230m depth. The 15,000m program aims to expand resources ahead of the definitive feasibility study due Q2 2026.

headline: K92 DELIVERS 46,743 oz AuEq in Q1 at 10.2 g/t Head Grade — 37% More Ore Processed Than Q1 2025
snippet: K92 Mining produced 46,743 oz AuEq from its Kainantu mine in Papua New Guinea in Q1 2026, at a head grade of 10.2 g/t Au — with ore processed up 37% vs. Q1 2025. Full-year 2026 guidance reaffirmed at 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq.

headline: KAMOA-KAKULA SHOCKER — Ivanhoe Cuts 2026 Copper Guidance ~20% After Seismic Event; Cash Costs Rise to $2.60–$3.00/lb
snippet: Ivanhoe Mines cut Kamoa-Kakula copper production guidance ~20% for both 2026 and 2027 following a May 2025 seismic event. 2026 guidance is now 380,000–420,000 tonnes; cash costs revised up to $2.60–$3.00/lb during the Kakula redevelopment period.

headline: Alphamin Posts RECORD US$158M Q1 EBITDA — Tin SURGES 30% to US$49,278/t Average, Up 46% vs. Prior Quarter
snippet: Alphamin Resources guided Q1 2026 EBITDA to a record US$158M, up 46% quarter-over-quarter, on the back of a 30% surge in tin prices to an average of US$49,278/t. The Bisie mine in the DRC produced 5,026 tonnes of tin in the quarter.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss With A$290M — Glencore Backs First New Australian Lithium Output Since 2023
snippet: Core Lithium greenlighted the restart of its Finniss lithium project with A$290M in funding, including a Glencore convertible note. Ore extraction begins May 2026 with first spodumene concentrate targeted for Q3 2026; nameplate capacity of 1.2 Mt/yr expected by mid-2028.

headline: Denison Breaks Ground on Phoenix ISR Uranium Mine — FIRST New Canadian Uranium Mine Since 2004
snippet: Denison Mines commenced construction on its Phoenix In-Situ Recovery uranium mine in Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan, following CNSC approval in February 2026. It is the first new Canadian uranium mine to enter construction since 2004, targeting first production by mid-2028.

headline: TRUMP TAKES 10% STAKE in USA Rare Earth — $1.6B Government Package Targets 40,000t/Day From Round Top by 2030
snippet: The Trump administration announced a $1.6B debt-and-equity investment in USA Rare Earth, taking a 10% government stake. The deal targets 40,000 metric tonnes per day of rare earth feedstock extraction from the Round Top deposit in Texas by 2030.

headline: RIO-GLENCORE $260B MEGA-MERGER COLLAPSES — Valuation Dispute Ends Third Attempt at Creating Mining Supermajor
snippet: Rio Tinto and Glencore ended their $260B merger talks after failing to bridge a valuation gap, with Glencore arguing Rio's offer undervalued its copper business. It marks the third failed combination attempt between the two miners, following earlier breakdowns in 2008 and 2014.

headline: Gold Miners FINALLY Outpacing the Metal — $3,000+/oz Operating Margins Emerge as Junior Miners Still Lag
snippet: With gold above $4,500/oz, senior miners are printing $3,000+ per ounce margins, generating record operating leverage. Newmont is up ~122% over the past year, yet junior miners remain significantly undervalued — analysts argue the catch-up trade is still in early innings.
